sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Fiorentina are struggling, sitting at the bottom of the Serie A table with no wins in their last 5 matches (2 draws, 3 losses), including a recent 2-1 victory over Dynamo Kyiv in Europe but a 3-1 loss to Sassuolo in the league. Hellas Verona, just above Fiorentina in 19th place, have shown slight improvement with a 3-1 win over Atalanta but have otherwise managed only 1 draw and 3 losses in their last 5, indicating a lack of consistency.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Fiorentina and Hellas Verona have split the results, with Fiorentina winning 3-1 at home in November 2024 and Verona edging a 1-0 victory in February 2025. Historically, Fiorentina have a slight edge with 3 wins in the last 5 head-to-heads, suggesting they might have a psychological advantage at home.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Fiorentina will rely on Rolando Mandragora (4 goals this season) to drive their attack, especially given their ability to create big chances despite a low conversion rate (28%). Hellas Verona’s Giovane, in form with 3 goals in the last 5 matches, poses a threat on the counter, supported by a defense that has struggled to keep clean sheets (none in last 3 games).

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Fiorentina’s defense is leaky, conceding 24 goals (19th in the league), while their attack has managed just 11 goals, overperforming expected goals by 7.75. Verona mirror these struggles with 21 goals conceded and 11 scored, but their recent attacking output suggests they can exploit Fiorentina’s defensive frailties.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction of a 45% chance for a Fiorentina win, 31% for a draw, and 24% for Verona, combined with Fiorentina’s home advantage and historical edge, a narrow home victory seems likely. However, both teams’ tendency to concede (both without clean sheets recently) and high card counts (over 4.5 cards in recent games) point to a scrappy, competitive match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Hellas Verona lead Fiorentina 1-0 at the Stadio Artemio Franchi. The visitors struck late in the first half through Gift Orban, capitalizing on one of their rare attacking opportunities. Fiorentina, despite dominating possession and creating numerous chances, have been unable to convert, leaving them trailing at the break. The home side will feel frustrated as they’ve controlled much of the game but lack the finishing touch, while Verona have been clinical with their limited openings.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Fiorentina's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Fiorentina have been the dominant force in the first half, holding the lion’s share of possession and frequently entering the final third. They’ve created multiple big chances but have been let down by poor finishing, missing several clear-cut opportunities. Players like Moise Kean have been heavily involved in attack but wasteful in front of goal. Defensively, they’ve been solid for the most part, though a lapse in concentration allowed Verona to take the lead. The home side will need to sharpen their finishing if they are to turn this game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Hellas Verona's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Hellas Verona have adopted a more defensive approach, conceding possession and focusing on counter-attacks. Their strategy paid off with a well-taken goal from Gift Orban just before the break, showcasing their ability to capitalize on limited chances. They’ve struggled to maintain consistent pressure in the final third and have been under siege for large parts of the game. Defensively, they’ve been resilient, with multiple clearances and a strong aerial presence, though their disciplinary record is a concern with several yellow cards already picked up.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Fiorentina dictate the tempo for the majority of the time, with consistent attacking spells especially in the early stages and towards the middle of the half. Their momentum was particularly strong during sustained periods of pressure, where they pushed Verona deep into their own half. Verona, on the other hand, had fewer attacking moments but showed flashes of danger during brief counter-attacking spells, with their momentum peaking just before scoring. Fiorentina’s inability to convert their attacking waves into goals has been their downfall so far, while Verona made the most of a rare surge forward.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Fiorentina, Moise Kean will be crucial in the second half as he’s already been involved in several chances and will be eager to rectify earlier misses. Nicolò Fagioli’s creativity in midfield, with key passes and chance creation, could unlock Verona’s defense if given space. On the Verona side, Gift Orban stands out as a constant threat after scoring the opener, and his ability to exploit spaces on the break will be vital. Mutassim Al-Musrati’s role in midfield, both in defense and setting up attacks, will also be key to maintaining Verona’s structure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Fiorentina are likely to come out with renewed intensity in the second half, pushing hard for an equalizer given their dominance in the first 45 minutes. They’ll need to be more clinical in front of goal and may look to exploit set-pieces with their corner count advantage. Verona, meanwhile, will probably stick to their defensive setup, aiming to protect their lead while looking for counter-attacking opportunities. Discipline will be critical for the visitors, as further bookings could disrupt their game plan. Expect Fiorentina to press high and test Verona’s resolve, but the away side’s organization could frustrate them if they remain compact.
